chapter_title.en: The Prayer for Eclipses, Earthquakes, Winds, and Oppression and Their Causes

text: : كُنْتُ مَعَ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ بِالْعُرَيْضِ فَهَبَّتْ رِيحٌ شَدِيدَةٌ فَجَعَلَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يُكَبِّرُ ثُمَّ قَالَ «إِنَّ اَلتَّكْبِيرَ يَرُدُّ اَلرِّيحَ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.1518 - t is narrated from Kamil, who said:
"I was with Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) in al-‘Urayd when a strong wind blew. Abu Ja'far (as) began to say 'Allahu Akbar' (Allah (swt) is the Greatest) repeatedly, and then said: 'Indeed, saying Takbir (Allahu Akbar) repels the wind.'"
